DECLARATION OF JOHN S. CHA IN SUPPORT OF DEFENDANTS? MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM DEFAULT JUDGMENT UNDER C.C.P. SECTION 473 (TRANSACTION ID # 100010933) FILED BY DEFENDANT 800 DEGREES WESTWOOD, LLC RAVERT, ALLEN
REPLY IN SUPPORT OF DEFENDANTS MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM DEFAULT JUDGMENT UNDER C.C.P. SECTION 473 (TRANSACTION ID # 100011764) FILED BY DEFENDANT 800 DEGREES WESTWOOD, LLC RAVERT, ALLEN

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS ALLEN RAVERT AND 800 DEGREES WESTWOOD, LLC'S MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM DEFAULT JUDGMENT UNDER C.C.P. SECTION 473 IS DENIED. THE EVIDENCE PRESENTED DOES NOT ESTABLISH THAT PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL'S CONDUCT AMOUNTED TO EXTRINSIC FRAUD OR LED TO AN EXTRINSIC MISTAKE BY DEFENDANTS' COUNSEL. MR. CHA CONTENDS THAT DEFENDANTS DID NOT TIMELY FILE AN ANSWER TO THE COMPLAINT AS THEY MISTAKENLY BELIEVED THAT MR. COOK WOULD WORK WITH MR. CHA TO INFORMALLY RESOLVE THE MATTER THROUGH OUT OF COURT NEGOTIATIONS. FROM SEPTEMBER 25, 2015, WHEN MR. CHA WAS HIRED, UNTIL NOVEMBER 23, 2015, WHEN DEFAULT WAS ENTERED, DEFENDANTS DID NOT FILE AN ANSWER OR ANY RESPONSIVE PLEADING. MR. CHA CLAIMS THAT BUT FOR MR. COOK'S ACTIONS, HE WOULD HAVE FILED AN ANSWER OR SOUGHT AN EXTENSION. HOWEVER, THE FACT THAT MR. COOK SAID HE WOULD PROVIDE AN ACCOUNTING DOES NOT CONSTITUTE FRAUD. THE FACT THAT HE DID NOT RESPOND TO ANY OF MR. CHA'S EMAILS AFTER SEPTEMBER 25, 2015, INDICATES THAT MR. CHA COULD NOT HAVE REASONABLY BELIEVED HE WAS IN NEGOTIATIONS WITH MR. COOK. ADDITIONALLY, MR. CHA CANNOT CLAIM THAT HE FAILED TO ANSWER BASED ON INADVERTENCE, PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 473 (B), BECAUSE THERE IS NOT EVIDENCE THAT THE PARTIES WERE IN ONGOING NEGOTIATIONS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

calendar.matters:
Defendants Allen Ravert, 800 Degrees Westwood, Llc'S Motion For Relief From Default Judgment Under C.C.P. Section 473
Off Calendar Jan-25-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ryan Dahm of Cook Collection Attorneys, PLC telephone number 415-989-4730 for Plaintiff.
Brandon Meads of Raines Feldman LLP telephone number 310-440-4100 for Defendants.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tenative ruling.
Defendants Allen Ravert and 800 Degrees Westwood, LLC'S Motion For Relief From Default Judgment Under C.C.P. Section 473 is denied. The evidence presented does not establish that Plaintiff's counsel's conduct amounted to extrinsic fraud or led to an extrinsic mistake by Defendants' counsel. Mr. Cha contends that Defendants did not timely file an answer to the complaint as they mistakenly believed that Mr. Cook would work with Mr. Cha to informally resolve the matter through out of court negotiations. From September 25, 2015, when Mr. Cha was hired, until November 23, 2015, when default was entered, Defendants did not file an answer or any responsive pleading. Mr. Cha claims that but for Mr. Cook's actions, he would have filed an answer or sought an extension. However, the fact that Mr. Cook said he would provide an accounting does not constitute fraud. The fact that he did not respond to any of Mr. Cha's emails after September 25, 2015, indicates that Mr. Cha could not have reasonably believed he was in negotiations with Mr. Cook. Additionally, Mr. Cha cannot claim that he failed to answer based on inadvertence, pursuant to CCP section 473 (b), because there is not evidence that the parties were in ongoing negotiations. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)
